zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus於大約1354年出生在蘇格蘭Forfarshire時，她的父親Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.24歲，母親Margaret Sinclair of Roslin28歲。她於1370年與Thomas 9th Earl of Mar在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪結婚。她去世於1417年5月23日在英國蘇格蘭安格斯享年64歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭安格斯福爾法爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus于大约1354年出生在苏格兰Forfarshire时，她的父亲Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.24岁，母亲Margaret Sinclair of Roslin28岁。她于1370年与Thomas 9th Earl of Mar在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪结婚。她去世于1417年5月23日在英国苏格兰安格斯享年64岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰安格斯福爾法爾。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus was born about 1354, in Forfarshire, Scotland, her father, Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus., was 24 and her mother, Margaret Sinclair of Roslin, was 28. She married Thomas 9th Earl of Mar in 1370, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. She died on 23 May 1417, in Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Forfar, Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus est née environ 1354, à Forfarshire, Écosse, son père, Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus., avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Sinclair of Roslin, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas 9th Earl of Mar en 1370, à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Elle est décédée le 23 mai 1417, à Angus, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Forfar, Angus, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus um 1354 in Forfarshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. 24 und ihre Mutter Margaret Sinclair of Roslin 28. Sie hat 1370 in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland Thomas 9th Earl of Mar geheiratet. Sie starb am 23. Mai 1417 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Angus, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Forfar, Angus, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus è nata il circa 1354 nel Forfarshire, Scotland, sua padre, Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus., aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret Sinclair of Roslin, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Thomas 9th Earl of Mar nel 1370, nel Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Morì il 23 maggio 1417, nel Angus, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Forfar, Angus, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart Countess of Angusが約1354年にスコットランドForfarshireで生まれた時，父Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.は24歳，母Margaret Sinclair of Roslinは28歳であった。1370年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンでThomas 9th Earl of Marと結婚した。彼女は1417年5月23日にイギリススコットランドアンガスで64歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアンガスフォーファルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus(이)가 대략 1354년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus.(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Sinclair of Roslin(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1370년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 Thomas 9th Earl of Mar(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1417년 5월 23일 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스 포파르에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas 9th Earl of Mar em 1370, em Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus родилась около 1354 г. в местности Forfarshire, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Thomas Stewart 2nd Earl of Angus. было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Sinclair of Roslin, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas 9th Earl of Mar в 1370 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия. Она умерла 23 мая 1417 г. в местности ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности форфар, ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания.
